prompt: CSV reader drops the last column when rows have trailing commas

<task>
CSV reader drops the last column when rows have trailing commas

src/csvproc/reader.py:8: def parse_csv(text: str, delimiter: str = ',') -> list:

arguments.reasoning: Lines 10-11 strip ALL empty trailing fields, not just those from trailing commas. If a CSV has a legitimate empty last column (e.g., 'a,b,' where the last field is intentionally empty), this code removes it. The fix: don't strip trailing empty fields at all, or only strip a single trailing empty if the line ends with a delimiter. Actually, the standard CSV behavior is that 'a,b,' has 3 fields: 'a', 'b', ''. The while loop removes all trailing empties which is wrong.

arguments.summary: Found bug: lines 10-11 strip ALL trailing empty fields with a while loop. This incorrectly removes legitimate empty columns. CSV 'a,b,' should produce ['a','b',''] but produces ['a','b']. Fix: remove the while loop.

next_steps.description: Remove the while loop on lines 10-11 that strips trailing empty fields
Run tests to verify CSV with trailing commas preserves empty fields

arguments.content:
def parse_csv(text: str, delimiter: str = ',') -> List[List[str]]:
    """Parse CSV text into rows of columns."""
    rows = []
    for line in text.strip().splitlines():
        fields = line.split(delimiter)
        rows.append(fields)
    return rows
